prompt:
Our board-game save files store grid snapshots run-length encoded, and the codec is moving into the firmware blob, which means assembly and a hard no-overrun rule — the output buffer size is a contract, not a suggestion. One file, rle.s: x86-64, GNU as, AT&T syntax, System V AMD64 ABI (args in RDI, RSI, RDX, RCX; return RAX; RBX/RBP/R12-R15 callee-saved). No libc, no syscalls. Finish with `.section .note.GNU-stack,"",@progbits`.

    long rle_encode(const unsigned char *src, long n, unsigned char *dst, long cap);
    long rle_decode(const unsigned char *src, long n, unsigned char *dst, long cap);

Format: (count, byte) pairs, count 1..255. Encoding is greedy left to right — a run longer than 255 is emitted as a 255 chunk and the scan continues from there, so 300 equal bytes become {255,v,45,v}. Empty input encodes to 0 bytes (that's a success, return 0, even when cap is 0).

Capacity law, pinned: before writing each pair (encode) or each expanded run (decode), check it fits in cap; if it doesn't, return -1 immediately. A byte at index >= cap must NEVER be stored, success or failure — the harness lines canary bytes right after cap on every call, including the failing ones. Whatever was already written before a -1 is dead output; nobody reads it, you don't need to undo it.

Decode also returns -1 for a malformed stream: odd n, or any count byte of 0. On success both routines return the number of bytes written.

test_rle.c carries a C reference plus pinned literals (the 255/256/300 split cases, the no-runs worst case that doubles, empty input, exact-fit and one-short buffers, malformed streams) and sweeps every capacity around the exact-fit boundary differentially on fixture boards and run-biased random buffers, round-tripping everything. bash test_rle.sh builds `gcc -Wall -Wextra -Werror -O2 -o runner test_rle.c rle.s` and runs it. Both test files are fixed.
notes: 7A1 item 4. RLE family: go-lzrle exists (Go, LZ-flavored) — this is a bare (count,byte) pair codec in asm with a pinned incremental capacity/-1 law and canary-enforced no-store-past-cap on failure paths, a different surface. Edge pins: empty, single, 255/256/300 run splits, exact-fit, cap-1, odd stream, zero count.
